title: Elementary School Musical

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Episode opening with Homer's signature exclamation
jokes.punchline: The familiar D'oh! that establishes Homer's presence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase that's become cultural touchstone, perfectly economical but lacks originality at this point

jokes.text: It's 4:00 a.m. You kids should have been in bed a half-hour ago.
jokes.setup: Parents telling kids they should have been in bed
jokes.punchline: At 4:00 AM, meaning bedtime was supposed to be 3:30 AM - absurdly late
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sets up normal parental concern then reveals the absurd timing

jokes.text: We're watching the Nobel Prize announcements live from Stockholm. Ooh, the Nobies!
jokes.setup: Family watching prestigious Nobel Prize ceremony
jokes.punchline: Bart calls them 'the Nobies' like a casual nickname for an awards show
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between Lisa's intellectualism and Bart's casual attitude toward high culture

jokes.text: Huzzah! I had him in the pool.
jokes.setup: Economics Nobel Prize winner announced
jokes.punchline: Lisa has a betting pool on Nobel Prize winners
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character comedy - her intellectual interests taken to absurd gambling extremes

jokes.text: I would kill for that.
jokes.setup: Discussion of the Nobel Peace Prize being the noblest
jokes.punchline: Lisa saying she'd kill for the Peace Prize
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic contradiction - violence for peace prize. Economical and darkly funny

jokes.text: Krusty the Clown wins Nobel Peace Prize
jokes.setup: Anticipation for prestigious Peace Prize winner
jokes.punchline: It goes to Krusty the Clown
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist premise that drives the episode - prestigious prize going to lowest-brow character

jokes.text: joining the ranks of Martin Luther King, Jr., Gandhi, and Desmond Tutu, here's the man who always parks in my spot
jokes.setup: Listing legendary peace leaders
jokes.punchline: Following with petty complaint about parking
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons high/low contrast with Kent's petty grievance amid historic context

jokes.text: this thing is worth nearly two million dollars. How do you like me now, Teen Choice Awards?
jokes.setup: Krusty receiving prestigious Nobel Peace Prize
jokes.punchline: He brags about the money and compares to Teen Choice Awards
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty - completely misses the honor's significance, focuses on money and petty comparisons

jokes.text: I'm looking forward to visiting the Arctic Circle, or as I call it, my wife's side of the bed! For the purposes of that joke, I'm married.
jokes.setup: Standard wife joke about cold bed
jokes.punchline: Meta admission that he's not actually married just for the joke
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy that acknowledges the artifice of stand-up marriage jokes while doing one

jokes.text: Hey! You! Big Chief Laugh-at-Crap.
jokes.setup: Krusty addressing Homer
jokes.punchline: Calls him 'Big Chief Laugh-at-Crap' - a play on Native American titles
jokes.explanation: Typical Krusty crude humor, characteristically appropriate but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Arctic Circle-- I just got that!
jokes.setup: Krusty's joke from earlier about cold wife's side of bed
jokes.punchline: Homer finally understanding the geography connection
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer slow-on-the-uptake character comedy, well-executed but predictable

jokes.text: Homer, you're the easiest laugh I know. How easy am I? Shut up!
jokes.setup: Krusty complimenting Homer as easy laugh
jokes.punchline: Homer eagerly asks follow-up, Krusty cuts him off
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Homer's eagerness for validation vs Krusty's impatience

jokes.text: Can my son come, too? He knows what to do when I swallow my tongue.
jokes.setup: Homer asking to bring Bart
jokes.punchline: Reason is medical emergency involving swallowing tongue
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer humor suggesting both his medical issues and Bart's competence in emergencies

jokes.text: They fight and bite? I never go to Europe without learning something.
jokes.setup: Hearing Itchy and Scratchy theme song lyrics
jokes.punchline: Homer treats this as educational European cultural insight
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer misunderstanding - finding profundity in cartoon violence lyrics

jokes.text: Your drugs put me to sleep. Your drugs wake me up. If I ask you to give me more, you say no. If I ask you again, you give 'em to me.
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining drug protocols to Homer and Bart
jokes.punchline: The casual way he describes his drug dependency system
jokes.explanation: Dark Krusty humor about his substance abuse, treated matter-of-factly

jokes.text: But no, Bart gets everything because he's the oldest, and he's a boy. And Maggie gets what's ever left over, because she's the youngest and she's a baby. Meanwhile, I just stand and wave to people going to better places.
jokes.setup: Lisa's middle child complaint
jokes.punchline: The imagery of standing and waving to people going better places
jokes.explanation: Relatable middle child syndrome with vivid, slightly pathetic imagery

jokes.text: Would half a bottle of confiscated water cheer you up? Ew, no thanks. Oh. Quite the little princess, ain't she?
jokes.setup: Security offering confiscated water as consolation
jokes.punchline: Lisa rejecting it and being called a princess for having standards
jokes.explanation: Decent airport security humor with Lisa's characteristic standards clashing with low expectations

jokes.text: I'm Ira Glass. Remember when you were five years old, putting ketchup on your hot dog? Today, in five acts: condiments. Act one, mustard. Act two, mayo. Act three, relish. Act four, corn relish...
jokes.setup: This American Life podcast playing
jokes.punchline: Perfect parody of Ira Glass's style applied to trivial condiment topic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of This American Life's earnest treatment of mundane topics

jokes.text: Oh, I get it. They go to Oslo, we get some ice cream.
jokes.setup: Lisa realizing the consolation plan
jokes.punchline: Bitter acceptance of ice cream as equivalent to Nobel Prize ceremony
jokes.explanation: Lisa's resignation and sarcasm about the massive disparity in treats

jokes.text: spend a week at performing arts camp!
jokes.setup: Lisa expecting ice cream as consolation
jokes.punchline: Actually getting performing arts camp - much better surprise
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection reveal, effective but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Whoops-- didn't time that quite right.
jokes.setup: Marge's big reveal moment
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of mistiming the surprise
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor about the mechanics of surprise reveals

jokes.text: Victory for the middle child!
jokes.setup: Lisa's earlier middle child complaints
jokes.punchline: Celebrating this as a middle child victory
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to her earlier grievances, turning triumph into vindication

jokes.text: Aw, you think I forgot you? Babies shouldn't jump to conclusions.
jokes.setup: Marge addressing baby Maggie
jokes.punchline: Treating a baby like she can understand complex advice about assumptions
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist humor about treating babies like rational adults

jokes.text: You're gonna like arts camp, Lisa / Marge paid with her debit VISA
jokes.setup: Inspirational camp song
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly working payment method into rhyme scheme
jokes.explanation: Funny forced rhyme that breaks the inspirational tone with mundane payment details

jokes.text: You said counselors twice. We do a lot of counseling. Yeah, but we don't do twice as much counseling. Well, don't question me in front of the children!
jokes.setup: Kurt listing his multiple job titles
jokes.punchline: Argument about whether saying counselors twice means twice as much counseling
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing their relationship through petty argument about semantics

jokes.text: She can't hear us, we're stage whispering. I can hear you. No, you can't.
jokes.setup: Counselors stage whispering
jokes.punchline: Insisting Lisa can't hear them even after she responds
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about theatrical conventions taken literally despite evidence

jokes.text: All our camp T-shirts are medium / Well, I hope you wear medium, girl-- huh!
jokes.setup: Getting Lisa a camp shirt
jokes.punchline: Singing about hoping her size matches their only available size
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about camp's limited resources expressed through unnecessary musical style

jokes.text: Do you guys always do that? Do what? Sing what you just said, then play a little guitar riff? No, we don't do that / We never do that / Well, we sometimes do that / But very infrequently, lady.
jokes.setup: Lisa questioning their constant singing
jokes.punchline: They deny it while doing exactly what she described
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-comedy about musical theater conventions, executed while demonstrating the very behavior being denied

jokes.text: How 'bout that airline food, huh? They should've called it 'chicken cordon blech'!
jokes.setup: Krusty doing standard airline food complaint
jokes.punchline: Weak pun on chicken cordon bleu
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad comedy - fits Krusty's hack persona with terrible airline food joke and weak pun

jokes.text: Wait a minute... This doesn't look like Oslo. Where are the wood-stave churches? Historic Akershus Fortress?
jokes.setup: Krusty expecting to be in Oslo for Nobel Prize
jokes.punchline: His surprising knowledge of specific Oslo landmarks
jokes.explanation: Good character surprise - Krusty showing unexpected cultural knowledge about Norwegian architecture

jokes.text: You are in Holland... specifically, The Hague. Did you say a Hague? I said The Hague.
jokes.setup: Official explaining they're in The Hague
jokes.punchline: Krusty mishearing 'The Hague' as 'a Hague'
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on proper noun vs. indefinite article, fits Krusty's confusion

jokes.text: It was merely a ruse to get you to Europe, so we could try you for the terrible crimes you've committed on this continent!
jokes.setup: Krusty confused about fake Nobel Prize
jokes.punchline: Elaborate international legal sting operation for comedy crimes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - international law enforcement focused on bad comedy performances

jokes.text: You dropped a monkey from the Eiffel Tower... In Greece, you committed something called 'aggravated hey-hey'... And here in Holland, you stole the entire act of our beloved clown 'Van Krusten.'
jokes.setup: Reading Krusty's international comedy crimes
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd and specific charges including 'aggravated hey-hey'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list with 'aggravated hey-hey' being particularly inspired legal comedy terminology

jokes.text: U bestolen al mijn jokenchortles!
jokes.setup: Dutch clown accusing Krusty of theft
jokes.punchline: Made-up Dutch words that sound like 'joke' and 'chortles'
jokes.explanation: Creative fake Dutch that sounds believable while incorporating English comedy terms

jokes.text: Your dog's dead! Um... that's not really a heckle.
jokes.setup: Heckler training exercise at arts camp
jokes.punchline: Student shouting devastating personal news instead of actual heckle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations - devastating news delivered as failed heckle attempt

jokes.text: I've seen more life in the Wellington Botanic Gardens! That is a heckle, but if you're not from New Zealand, it loses some of its sting.
jokes.setup: Student attempting another heckle
jokes.punchline: Overly specific New Zealand reference that defeats its own purpose
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on how overly specific references undermine insults

jokes.text: If they did know the Gardens, then they would know that they're teeming with life.
jokes.setup: Continuing analysis of the New Zealand heckle
jokes.punchline: The heckle is backwards - the gardens are actually full of life
jokes.explanation: Logical follow-up that compounds the failure of the original heckle attempt

jokes.text: We're highly successful artists. And as artists, we live in Springfield's most artistic borough... Sprooklyn.
jokes.setup: Camp counselors describing their artistic lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Living in 'Sprooklyn' - Springfield's version of Brooklyn
jokes.explanation: Good portmanteau joke playing on artistic pretensions and Brooklyn's creative reputation

jokes.text: playing my Gibson... rehearsing my Ibsen.
jokes.setup: Listing their artistic activities
jokes.punchline: Gibson guitar and Ibsen plays rhyming coincidentally
jokes.explanation: Nice coincidental rhyme that fits their pretentious artist characters

jokes.text: Three layers, Mother Nature.
jokes.setup: Describing s'mores making
jokes.punchline: Addressing Mother Nature directly about s'mores technique
jokes.explanation: Mild pretentious artist humor about treating camping as performance art

jokes.text: Excuse me while I talk to this cow that is grazing / I give art like you give milk
jokes.setup: Lisa joining the artistic song
jokes.punchline: Talking to a cow and comparing art creation to milk production
jokes.explanation: Lisa getting caught up in the artistic pretension with absurd cow comparison

jokes.text: 'Cause artists are smarter than Mr. Stephen Hawking
jokes.setup: Song about how great artists are
jokes.punchline: Claiming to be smarter than Stephen Hawking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of artistic pretension to absurd intellectual claims

jokes.text: To the gritty city when you're less itty-bitty, girl / The city's pretty gritty for an itty-bitty pretty girl.
jokes.setup: Encouraging Lisa to move to the city when older
jokes.punchline: Tongue-twisting rhyme scheme with itty-bitty-gritty-pretty
jokes.explanation: Competent musical wordplay with alliteration and internal rhyme, but not exceptional

jokes.text: Everyone's windmill needs a new blade now and then, right?
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to understand how to earn freedom
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding the situation as sexual proposition involving windmills
jokes.explanation: Krusty's crude misinterpretation fits his character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: No! Our windmills are the envy of the world!
jokes.setup: Krusty's sexual windmill comment
jokes.punchline: Dutch pride in their actual windmills completely missing the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good cultural pride joke that completely bypasses the innuendo with sincere windmill pride

jokes.text: Did you ever see my 'Wyatt Burp' sketch? Everyone on my payroll says it's great.
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to prove cultural contribution
jokes.punchline: Weak Western pun and admission that only paid employees praise it
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad Krusty material with self-aware admission of poor quality

jokes.text: Let me do my 'Dutch Oven' bit! It's about other Dutchmen, not you.
jokes.setup: Krusty offering another comedy bit
jokes.punchline: Dutch Oven joke with awkward clarification about the subject matter
jokes.explanation: More deliberately bad Krusty comedy with crude humor and poor timing

jokes.text: Those clouds look like a casting director saying, 'You've got the part.'
jokes.setup: Lisa at arts camp looking at clouds
jokes.punchline: Seeing theater-related shapes in clouds instead of typical animals
jokes.explanation: Nice character moment showing Lisa's new theatrical mindset affecting her perception

jokes.text: That cloud looks like legendary Broadway lyricist Betty Comden.
jokes.setup: Continuing cloud watching
jokes.punchline: Seeing specific Broadway figure in clouds - very niche reference
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's deep dive into theater knowledge with obscure but real Broadway reference

jokes.text: Monday was orientation. Tuesday, we did mime. Wednesday we did Mame. Thursday we got attacked by bees.
jokes.setup: Lisa recounting her camp week
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of bee attack among normal camp activities
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of escalating camp experiences with bee attack as throwaway line

jokes.text: Friday morning, Andrew Lloyd Webber and Elaine Stritch helped us make wallets.
jokes.setup: Continuing Friday activities list
jokes.punchline: Major Broadway figures doing mundane craft activities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image of famous composers doing summer camp crafts

jokes.text: Angels in America: A Gay Fantasia on National Themes. Part One: Millennium Approaches. Part Two... Perestroika? That's right!
jokes.setup: Lisa reciting the full title of their ambitious play
jokes.punchline: Kurt's proud confirmation of Lisa's complete recall of the complex title
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's absorption of high theater culture and counselor's pride

jokes.text: I'll miss you a lot, then a little, then not too much.
jokes.setup: Kurt saying goodbye to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest timeline of diminishing affection
jokes.explanation: Perfectly structured emotional arc from sentiment to brutal honesty about forgetting people

jokes.text: I don't want to get stuck behind the joggers from the fat camp.
jokes.setup: Lisa needing to leave quickly
jokes.punchline: Concern about slow-moving fat camp joggers blocking her path
jokes.explanation: Mild dark humor about fat camp logistics, somewhat mean-spirited but fits context

jokes.text: Oh, you didn't see a boy lose his swim trunks, did you?
jokes.setup: Lisa saying something completely changed her
jokes.punchline: Marge's assumption it was an embarrassing moment rather than artistic awakening
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - practical mother missing the deeper meaning for physical explanation

jokes.text: We've got plenty of songs in the car, by Maggie's favorite singer: Roofi.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting to continue with music and theater
jokes.punchline: Marge offering children's music as equivalent to Lisa's artistic interests
jokes.explanation: Parody of Raffi and parent disconnect between high art and children's entertainment

jokes.text: Brush your teeth, comb your hair / Have an apple or a pear... Bup-up-up. Safety locks.
jokes.setup: Roofi's wholesome children's song playing
jokes.punchline: Marge engaging safety locks to prevent Lisa's escape during song
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Lisa's artistic imprisonment with wholesome children's music

jokes.text: Sorry. I guess I shouldn't be playing this driving game while I'm driving.
jokes.setup: Marge driving erratically
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to playing driving video game while actually driving
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Marge moment with dangerous gaming while driving

jokes.text: Hey Lisa, how was farts camp? Farts camp-- that's great.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about Lisa's arts camp experience
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'arts' as 'farts' and being pleased with his joke
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart juvenile humor, perfectly in character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Did you paint a picture on toilet paper?
jokes.setup: Continuing to mock Lisa's arts camp
jokes.punchline: Combining art with bathroom humor in his question
jokes.explanation: More Bart bathroom humor mixed with art mockery, in character but predictable

jokes.text: Lisa, I went to arts camp, too. And all it does is fill your coconut with banana-cream dreams that can never come true.
jokes.setup: Lisa's enthusiasm for arts camp
jokes.punchline: Bitter teacher's metaphor about unrealistic artistic dreams
jokes.explanation: Great metaphor combining head/coconut with specific pie flavoring for broken dreams

jokes.text: How do you cope? Vanilla Wafers, plum wine and take it from the top.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking how teacher deals with disappointment
jokes.punchline: Specific coping mechanism of cookies, alcohol, and repetition
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny specific breakdown of a failed artist's daily survival routine

jokes.text: My country 'tis of thee / Life disappointed me / I'm 53.
jokes.setup: Starting patriotic song
jokes.punchline: Turning it into personal disappointment anthem with age revelation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of patriotic song into personal lament, perfectly economical and devastating

jokes.text: Top to bottom, left to right, readin' things is outta sight.
jokes.setup: Looking through Krusty's old material for cultural contribution
jokes.punchline: His reading PSA with forced rhyme and dated slang
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad educational PSA material that fits Krusty's hack reputation

jokes.text: Speaking of out of sight, you've gotta hide me. I slept with the lighting director's wife, and...
jokes.setup: Krusty's reading PSA using 'out of sight'
jokes.punchline: Using the phrase to transition to needing to hide from affair consequences
jokes.explanation: Good transition using callback to connect educational material to personal scandal

jokes.text: One night we spoke only in lines from famous poems. Because I could not stop for death, he kindly stopped for me. Emily Dickinson. No, I was just describing my day.
jokes.setup: Lisa describing sophisticated camp conversations using poetry
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks she's still doing it when she's actually describing her mundane day
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection where Homer mistakes Lisa's regular speech for continued poetry exercise

jokes.text: My dream is to be able to walk up stairs like an eight-year-old girl.
jokes.setup: Lisa talking about giving up on her dreams
jokes.punchline: Homer's much simpler, physical aspiration
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contrast - where others have lofty dreams, he wants basic mobility

jokes.text: We'll take the town by storm, like the Bronte sisters.
jokes.setup: Lisa promising to send for Maggie when famous
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself and baby sister to famous literary siblings
jokes.explanation: Lisa's intellectual reference applied to her relationship with baby Maggie

jokes.text: Oh, Maggie, don't ever turn two.
jokes.setup: Lisa's emotional goodbye to baby sister
jokes.punchline: Wanting Maggie to stay a baby forever for innocence/cuteness
jokes.explanation: Sweet sentiment with mild humor about wanting to preserve childhood innocence

jokes.text: Brrr! Boy, it sure is cold in here. No heat. It's, uh, part of our art.
jokes.setup: Lisa noticing the cold apartment
jokes.punchline: Kurt pretending poverty conditions are artistic choice
jokes.explanation: Good artist pretension joke covering up poverty with claims of artistic integrity

jokes.text: Sandwich artists. But we get to eat any sandwiches we drop on the floor. Unless we drop them on purpose. They've got cameras on us at all times.
jokes.setup: Revealing their real jobs after artistic pretensions
jokes.punchline: Specific rules about dropped sandwich consumption under surveillance
jokes.explanation: Great deflation of artistic pretensions with specific workplace surveillance details

jokes.text: Look, Lisa, I think it's time we can sang you the truth.
jokes.setup: About to reveal the truth to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Malapropism combining 'can tell' and 'sang' showing their limited education
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing their pretensions exceed their actual education level

jokes.text: Artists are the least important people in the world / So whatever you do, don't be an artist, girl...
jokes.setup: Counter-song to their earlier artist celebration
jokes.punchline: Complete reversal of their previous message about artist importance
jokes.explanation: Effective reversal song structure mirroring their earlier enthusiasm with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Artists make a living dressing up like a falafel
jokes.setup: Continuing their anti-artist song
jokes.punchline: Specific humiliating costume job for struggling artists
jokes.explanation: Good specific example of degrading work artists must do to survive

jokes.text: You ain't no falafel!
jokes.setup: Song about dressing as falafel
jokes.punchline: Someone apparently yelling at a falafel costume person
jokes.explanation: Quick throwaway gag suggesting the falafel costume scenario is actually happening nearby

jokes.text: Artists sit in jail, on a lifetime hitch / The only way out is become a prison snitch
jokes.setup: Continuing brutal artist reality song
jokes.punchline: Prison time and snitching as artist career path
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation of artist struggles into criminal justice system

jokes.text: Oh, why did I sing that out loud?
jokes.setup: Just sang about becoming a prison snitch
jokes.punchline: Realizing he shouldn't have admitted to being a snitch in song
jokes.explanation: Good meta-moment about how musical theater compels truth-telling even when dangerous

jokes.text: We wanted you to think / We were happening guys. Well, philosophically, we're happening, in the sense that we exist.
jokes.setup: Explaining their deception about being successful artists
jokes.punchline: Philosophical defense of 'happening' meaning merely existing
jokes.explanation: Clever philosophical wordplay to justify their deception about success level

jokes.text: Come on, I thought you Euro-jerks loved unfunny comedy. We don't put 'Euro' in front of everything. Euro-guards! Take him away in Euro-cuffs.
jokes.setup: Krusty insulting European sense of humor
jokes.punchline: Official denying Euro-prefix usage while immediately using it twice
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and immediate contradiction showing the official doing exactly what he denies

jokes.text: Is it region one or region two? Uh, what region is America? Region one. Woo-hoo! We're region one!
jokes.setup: Question about DVD region compatibility
jokes.punchline: Homer and Bart celebrating being region one as if it's an achievement
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Homer finding national pride in arbitrary technical standards

jokes.text: Well, this is region two, so I'm afraid your exculpatory evidence will produce nothing more than an error message stating 'unreadable disc.'
jokes.setup: DVD region incompatibility issue
jokes.punchline: International legal proceedings failing due to DVD region locks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise of international justice system thwarted by DRM technology

jokes.text: If it please the court, my brother has a multi-region player. I just pray he's not on a state-mandated five-week vacation.
jokes.setup: Homer offering solution to DVD region problem
jokes.punchline: Specific European vacation policy potentially preventing legal proceeding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of DVD player as legal solution with European social policy detail

jokes.text: Get this back to me by 6:00. I'm having a Real Housewives of Atlanta season two party.
jokes.setup: Brother lending multi-region player for urgent legal case
jokes.punchline: Time pressure due to reality TV viewing party
jokes.explanation: Mild contrast between international legal urgency and trashy TV social priorities

jokes.text: My contract specifically stated half regular, half ruffled! Forget it! The gig's off! I ain't gonna play Sun City. Vuvuzela me out of here.
jokes.setup: Krusty's prima donna behavior about potato chips
jokes.punchline: Canceling South African apartheid-era performance over snack specifications
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from petty rider demand to historical consequence with vuvuzela reference

jokes.text: Three days later, South Africa freed Nelson Mandela from prison. Krusty the Klown, your selfishness has saved the world.
jokes.setup: Krusty's petty cancellation
jokes.punchline: His selfish act accidentally causing major historical liberation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant historical irony - Krusty's pettiness accidentally creating major positive change

jokes.text: Now, where's the nearest place I can score a little victory weed? That would be the courthouse cafeteria. I'll be right there. Order me, um, a raspberry crepe and a brick of hash.
jokes.setup: Krusty asking about marijuana after his legal victory
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of drug purchases at courthouse cafeteria
jokes.explanation: Standard drug humor with Dutch tolerance stereotype, moderately funny but not exceptional

jokes.text: How do you smoke it? I melt it and inject it into my eyelid. Hey, you're all right.
jokes.setup: Question about hash consumption method
jokes.punchline: Absurd injection method and casual acceptance
jokes.explanation: Absurdist drug humor that's moderately funny but relies on shock value over cleverness

jokes.text: What's she doing with her hands? It's called applause. I'm not sure I like it.
jokes.setup: Lisa clapping for their street performance
jokes.punchline: They don't understand applause and are suspicious of appreciation
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing they're so failed as artists they don't recognize positive audience response

jokes.text: Hi. I'm Moe. It's good that little kid left, 'cause this song is gonna get into a very dark area.
jokes.setup: Moe appearing on stage after Lisa leaves
jokes.punchline: Warning that his performance will be inappropriate for children
jokes.explanation: Good Moe character moment with self-aware warning about his dark content

jokes.text: Remember us for our exaggerations, not our reality.
jokes.setup: Final goodbye to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Requesting to be remembered for their lies rather than truth
jokes.explanation: Beautifully crafted line about preferring the fantasy version of themselves over reality

jokes.text: Is it your laundry? Okay, two surprises.
jokes.setup: Being offered a surprise gift
jokes.punchline: Correctly guessing one gift is just their dirty laundry
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about their poverty extending to gifting their own laundry

jokes.text: In a way, I think we learned more from her than she did from us. Yeah, well, obviously, because we taught her nothing.
jokes.setup: Typical teacher sentiment about learning from students
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty that they provided no educational value
jokes.explanation: Perfect subversion of cliched teacher wisdom with matter-of-fact admission of incompetence

jokes.text: How did we get way up here? Weren't we just back there? The magic of art.
jokes.setup: Lisa noticing inconsistent stage geography
jokes.punchline: Explaining continuity error as 'magic of art'
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor about stage/TV geography inconsistencies covered by artistic pretension

tmdb_overview: Marge sends Lisa to a performing arts camp, where counselors and fellow campers inspire her to embrace her creative side; upon returning to Springfield, Lisa grows restless with her boring suburban lifestyle.
